originalAlarm.description: `__eckey_814` could be null (null value originating from line 1356) and is dereferenced.

sanitizedDescription: `eckey` could be null (null value originating from line 1356) and is dereferenced.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 35ae2e259e4.c L12: eckey null pointer dereference — same bug as TRANSFORMATION_45/125/138. eckey from malloc() can be NULL; *eckey=30 at L12 is a null deref. PC: !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc AND !__USE_EXTERN_INLINES AND HAVE_ECC. The restrictive !_STDLIB_H over-constrains the PC. HAVE_ECC is the real feature flag. New bug, over-constrained PC.
